---
title: "Kimura Plots for Repeat Family (RF) Content Visualization"
site: workflowr::wflow_site
output:
  workflowr::wflow_html:
    toc: false
    toc_float: true
    toc_collapsed: false
    theme: default
    css: "theme3.css"
toc_depth: 1
number_sections: true
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

### RF Content Visualization & Analysis {.tabset}

#### File Preparation


To generate a kimura repeat content plot, first you must run the script included in the RepeatMasker module called *calcDivergenceFromAlign.pl*. I did this using the script below on the TAMU Grace Cluster

```{r, eval=FALSE}

#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=Generate-Dist
#SBATCH --time=03:00:00
#SBATCH --nodes=1          # max 64 nodes for partition long
#SBATCH --ntasks-per-node=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=48
#SBATCH --mem=360G
#SBATCH --mail-type=ALL
#SBATCH --mail-user=user@mail.com
#SBATCH --output=stdout.%x.%j
#SBATCH --error=stderr.%x.%j

module purge
module load GCC/11.3.0 OpenMPI/4.1.4 RepeatMasker/4.1.4

/sw/eb/sw/RepeatMasker/4.1.4-foss-2022a/util/calcDivergenceFromAlign.pl -s {SpeciesName}.distance {SpeciesName}_genomic.fa.align

```

This will generate a file with a *.distance* suffix. I find it easier to just download this file and generate the figure locally, as we will need to edit this file as well. When you open your *species.distance* file you should see something like in the block below:

```{r, eval=FALSE, attr.source='.numberLines'}
Jukes/Cantor and Kimura subsitution levels adjusted for CpG sites
=================================================================
File: GCF_023864275.1_iqSchCanc2.1_genomic.fa.align
Weighted average Kimura divergence for each repeat family
Class	Repeat	absLen	wellCharLen	Kimura%
-----	------	------	-----------	-------
DNA	rnd-4_family-3901	2850838	2828840	9.37
DNA	rnd-5_family-7626	384874	375022	18.86
DNA/Academ-1	rnd-5_family-132	136309	134796	21.75
⋮
LINE	rnd-4_family-267	2208155	2149395	32.22
LINE	rnd-4_family-268	243936	240416	20.67
LINE	rnd-5_family-1066	674334	658113	22.96
⋮
LTR/Copia	rnd-5_family-1055	766978	760738	15.83
LTR/Copia	rnd-5_family-1977	149895	146399	25.27
⋮
RC/Helitron	rnd-4_family-1433	1591130	1508796	17.81
RC/Helitron	rnd-4_family-1977	1352003	1334534	17.66
⋮
Simple_repeat	combined	45799202	45402746	----
Unknown	rnd-1_family-1	141049217	140819533	5.19
⋮
tRNA	rnd-5_family-26180	476868	476755	3.98
tRNA	rnd-5_family-5841	2251189	2236163	10.50


Coverage for each repeat class and divergence (Kimura)
Div DNA DNA/Academ-1 ... LINE LINE/CR1 ... LTR/Copia ... RC/Helitron Simple_repeat Unknown tRNA
0 290 2240 223288 ... 214984 707342 328 ... 6524  711 0 56168370 340641 
⋮
```

But much, much larger. Copy and paste from where it starts with "Div", below the header for the Kimura scores. Create a new file named {SpeciesSuffix}.distance and copy and paste the text to this file. It should look like this:

```{r, eval=FALSE}
Div DNA DNA/Academ-1 ... LINE LINE/CR1 ... LTR/Copia ... RC/Helitron Simple_repeat Unknown tRNA
0 290 2240 223288 ... 214984 707342 328 ... 6524  711 0 56168370 340641 
⋮
```

#### Major RFs: Single Genome


![](assets/simple_single_kimura.png)

To create a Kimura plot for a single genome, create a file called *kimura-single.R* and copy and paste the following code into it:
```{r, eval=FALSE}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #
# Repeat annotation summary
#
# Simple figure to summarise repeat families within each genome assembly

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #
# Libraries
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(magrittr)
  library(here)
  library(scales)
  library(ragg)
  library(readr)
})

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #

# ------------------- Edit for your species ------------------------------------------------- #

size = 8500000000 # Total genome size
sample_name = "Schistocerca cancellata"
file <- "song_assembly/SchCanc.distance"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #

genome_sizes <- tibble(sample = sample_name, size = size)
pal <- colorRampPalette(RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(n = 7, 'Set3')) # Set color pallette
header <- read_lines(file=file, n_max=1)

df.kimura <- read_table(file, col_types = cols(.default = col_double())) # Read in data (1 species)
df.kimura <- df.kimura[, -ncol(df.kimura)] %>% # Remove last column created from whitespace on EOL
  mutate(sample = sample_name) %>% # Add in col for sample name, unnecessary?
  left_join(genome_sizes, by = "sample") %>% # Join df.kimura with genome.sizes by sample
   pivot_longer(cols = -c(sample, size, Div), names_to = "repeat_type", values_to = "value")  %>%# Make the width of the column the height
   separate(col = repeat_type, into = c("Family", "Sub-family"), sep = "/")  %>% # Seperate Family and Sub-family by "/"
   mutate(`Sub-family` = ifelse(is.na(`Sub-family`), Family, `Sub-family`)) %>% # If sub-family empty, put Family name
  mutate(
    percentage = (value/size) * 100 # calculate percentage composition of genome
    ) %>%
    mutate(`Sub-family` = ifelse(is.na(`Sub-family`), Family, `Sub-family`))

tmp <- df.kimura |>
  filter(!is.na(value), !str_detect(Family, '\\?')) |>
  group_by(sample, Div, Family) |>
  mutate(total_family = sum(value), percentage_family = (total_family/size) * 100) |>
  select(sample, Div, Family, percentage_family) |>
  ungroup() |>
  distinct()

tmp_filtered <- tmp |> filter(percentage_family > 0.01)
 # Plotting
kimura.gg <- ggplot(tmp_filtered, aes(x = Div, y = percentage_family, fill = Family)) +
  geom_bar(position = "stack", stat = "identity", alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual( # associate colors with family names
    values = c(
      DNA = '#4eaf49',
      LINE = '#984f9f',
      LTR = '#397fb9',
      MITE = '#f7941d',
      Unknown = '#737e76'
    ),
    labels = c(
      DNA = "DNA",
      LINE = "LINEs",
      LTR = "LTRs",
      MITE = "MITEs",
      Unknown = "Unknown Repeats"
    )  ) +
  labs(
    x = '\nKimura substitution level',
    y = 'Genome percentage\n',
    fill = '\n'
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_number(suffix = '%')) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, 50, 5),
    limits = c(0, 55),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 51)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 20, face = 'bold'),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 18),
    legend.position = 'right',
    legend.title = element_text(size = 18),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 18),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 20, face = 'bold.italic')
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~sample, nrow = 4)

# Save plot
pdf(
  file = paste0(sample_name, "-kimura.pdf"),
  width = 12,
  height = 9
)
print(kimura.gg)
invisible(dev.off())
```



#### Specific RFs: Single Genome 
![](assets/complex_single_kimura.png)

To create the more complex style Kimura plot for a single genome, create a file called *kimura-single-complex.R* and copy and paste the below code into it:

<mark>Please note that this script uses manually assigned colors for each sub-family, as to assure consistency across different species' plots. You will likely need to generate your own color mapping based on the various repeat groups that are present in your genome.</mark>

```{r, eval=FALSE}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #
# Repeat annotation summary - consistent subfamily colors
#
# This script reads a .distance file, calculates subfamily genome percentages,
# and plots a stacked Kimura plot with consistent subfamily colors across species.
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(magrittr)
  library(readr)
  library(scales)
})

# ------------------- Edit for your species ------------------------------------------------- #
size <- 9100000000          # Total genome size
sample_name <- "Schistocerca serialis cubense"
file <- "song_assembly/SchGreg.distance"
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

# Define a fixed subfamily palette
subfamily_palette <- c(
  "DNA" = "#4eaf49", "DNA/Academ-1" = "#ffb703", "DNA/CMC-Chapaev-3" = "#984f9f",
  "DNA/CMC-EnSpm" = "#b57db5", "DNA/CMC-Transib" = "#fb8500", "DNA/Kolobok-Hydra" = "#6fa7d6",
  "DNA/MULE-MuDR" = "#f7941d", "DNA/MULE-NOF" = "#737e76", "DNA/Maverick" = "#90e0ef",
  "DNA/Merlin" = "#ffa69e", "DNA/P" = "#00b4d8", "DNA/PIF-Harbinger" = "#d1495b",
  "DNA/PiggyBac" = "#6ecf6e", "DNA/Sola-1" = "#ff595e", "DNA/Sola-2" = "#f94144",
  "DNA/TcMar-Fot1" = "#f3722c", "DNA/TcMar-Mariner" = "#f8961e", "DNA/TcMar-Pogo" = "#f9c74f",
  "DNA/TcMar-Tc1" = "#90be6d", "DNA/TcMar-Tc2" = "#43aa8b", "DNA/TcMar-Tc4" = "#577590",
  "DNA/TcMar-Tigger" = "#277da1", "LINE" = "#4e79a7", "LINE/CR1" = "#f28e2b",
  "LINE/Dong-R4" = "#e15759", "LINE/I" = "#76b7b2", "LINE/I-Jockey" = "#59a14f",
  "LINE/L1-Tx1" = "#edc948", "LINE/L2" = "#b07aa1", "LINE/Penelope" = "#ff9da7",
  "LINE/R1" = "#9c755f", "LINE/R1-LOA" = "#bab0ac", "LINE/R2" = "#d37295",
  "LINE/RTE-BovB" = "#fabfd2", "LINE/RTE-RTE" = "#b0b0b0", "LINE/RTE-X" = "#d4a6c8",
  "LINE/Rex-Babar" = "#7f7f7f", "LTR" = "#59c9a5", "LTR/Copia" = "#ff6f59",
  "LTR/Gypsy" = "#1982c4", "LTR/Pao" = "#ffca3a", "RC/Helitron" = "#8ac926",
  "Simple_repeat" = "#6a4c93", "Unknown" = "#397fb9", "tRNA" = "#ffca3a"
)

# ------------------- Read and process .distance file ---------------------------------------- #
df.kimura <- read_table(file, col_types = cols(.default = col_double()))

# Remove any trailing empty column (from whitespace)
if (colnames(df.kimura)[ncol(df.kimura)] == "") df.kimura <- df.kimura[, -ncol(df.kimura)]

# Add sample info
df.kimura <- df.kimura %>%
  mutate(sample = sample_name, size = size) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(sample, size, Div),
    names_to = "Sub-family",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Extract top-level family (optional)
    Family = ifelse(str_detect(`Sub-family`, "/"),
                    str_extract(`Sub-family`, "^[^/]+"),
                    `Sub-family`),
    # Clean whitespace
    `Sub-family` = str_trim(`Sub-family`),
    percentage_subfamily = (value / size) * 100
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(value), percentage_subfamily > 0.01)

# Keep only subfamilies present in palette
df.kimura <- df.kimura %>%
  filter(`Sub-family` %in% names(subfamily_palette)) %>%
  mutate(`Sub-family` = factor(`Sub-family`, levels = names(subfamily_palette)))

# ------------------- Plot --------------------------------------------------------------- #
kimura.gg <- ggplot(df.kimura, aes(x = Div, y = percentage_subfamily, fill = `Sub-family`)) +
  geom_bar(position = "stack", stat = "identity", alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = subfamily_palette) +
  labs(
    x = "Kimura substitution level",
    y = "Genome percentage",
    fill = "Sub-family"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_number(suffix = "%"), limits = c(0, 5)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 50, 5), limits = c(0, 55), expand = c(0,0)) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 51)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 18),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 18),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 14),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold.italic")
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~sample, nrow = 4)

# ------------------- Save plot ---------------------------------------------------------- #
pdf(paste0(sample_name, "-kimura-subfamily.pdf"), width = 12, height = 9)
print(kimura.gg)
invisible(dev.off())


```
#### Major RFs: Multi-genome

![](assets/simple_multi_kimura.png)
  
Create a new R script called *kimura-plot-mult-genomes.R* and copy and paste the following script into it:

```{r, eval=FALSE}
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #
# Repeat annotation summary
#
# Simple figure to summarise repeat families within each genome assembly

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #
# Librariesx
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(magrittr)
  library(here)
  library(scales)
  library(ragg)
})

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #

#########################################################################################################################
################################################# CHANGE HERE 1/5 ########################################################
genome.sizes <- tibble(
  sample = c('Schistocerca gregaria', 'Schistocerca piceifrons', 'Schistocerca cancellata', 'Schistocerca nitens', 'Schistocerca serialis cubense', 'Schistocerca americana'),
  size = c(8700000000, 8700000000, 8500000000, 8800000000, 9100000000, 9000000000)
)
##########################################################################################################################
##########################################################################################################################

pal <- colorRampPalette(RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(n = 6, 'Set3'))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #
# Kimura divergence tables
df.kimura <- fs::dir_ls(
  path = 'song_assembly',
  glob = '*distance',
  recurse = TRUE
) |>
  map(read_delim, delim = ' ', col_names = TRUE, col_types = cols()) |>
  list_rbind(names_to = 'sample') |>########################################################################################
  mutate( ############################################ CHANGE HERE 2/5 #######################################################
    sample = case_when(
      sample == 'song_assembly/SchGreg.distance' ~ 'Schistocerca gregaria',
      sample == 'song_assembly/SchPice.distance' ~ 'Schistocerca piceifrons',
      sample == 'song_assembly/SchCanc.distance' ~ 'Schistocerca cancellata',
      sample == 'song_assembly/SchNite.distance' ~ 'Schistocerca nitens',
      sample == 'song_assembly/SchSeri.distance' ~ 'Schistocerca serialis cubense',
      sample == 'song_assembly/SchAmer.distance' ~ 'Schistocerca americana'
    )#########################################################################################################################
  ) |>########################################################################################################################
  select(-starts_with('...')) |>
  (\(x) pivot_longer(data = x, names_to = 'repeat_type', values_to = 'value', cols = 3:ncol(x)))() |>
  left_join(genome.sizes, by = "sample")|>
  mutate(
    percentage = (value/size) * 100,################################# CHANGE HERE 3/5 #######################################################
    sample = factor(sample, levels = c('Schistocerca gregaria', 'Schistocerca piceifrons', 'Schistocerca cancellata', 'Schistocerca nitens', 'Schistocerca serialis cubense', 'Schistocerca americana'))
    ) |>####################################################################################################################################
  separate(col = repeat_type, into = c('Family', 'Sub-family'), sep = '/') |>
  mutate(`Sub-family` = ifelse(is.na(`Sub-family`), Family, `Sub-family`))

# Temporary while I re-run repeat masker with repbase
tmp <- df.kimura |>
  filter(!is.na(value), !str_detect(Family, '\\?')) |>
  group_by(sample, Div, Family) |>
  mutate(total_family = sum(value), percentage_family = (total_family/size) * 100) |>
  select(sample, Div, Family, percentage_family) |>
  ungroup() |>
  distinct()

tmp <- tmp %>% filter(!is.na(sample))


# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(scales)
library(patchwork)

# Now plot the rest (not gregaria) in 2 columns
other_plot <- tmp %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Div, y = percentage_family, fill = Family)) +
  geom_bar(position = "stack", stat = "identity", alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(DNA = '#4eaf49', LINE = '#984f9f', LTR = '#397fb9', MITE = '#f7941d', Unknown = '#737e76'),
    labels = c(DNA = "DNA", LINE = "LINEs", LTR = "LTRs", MITE = "MITEs", Unknown = "Unknown Repeats")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = '\nKimura substitution level',
    y = 'Genome percentage\n',
    fill = '\n'
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_number(suffix = '%')) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 50, 5), limits = c(0, 55), expand = c(0,0)) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 51)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 20, face = 'bold'),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 18),
    legend.position = 'bottom',
    legend.title = element_text(size = 18),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 18),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 20, face = 'bold.italic')
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~sample, ncol = 2)   # 2 columns, 3 rows automatically


# Combine the plots
combined <- other_plot + plot_layout(ncol=2)

# Save
pdf(file = here("multigenome-kimura.pdf"), width = 18, height = 25)
print(other_plot)
invisible(dev.off())




```



#### Specific RFs: Multi Genome
![](assets/complex_multi_kimura.png)

<mark>Please note that this script uses manually assigned colors for each sub-family, as to assure consistency across different species' plots. You will likely need to generate your own color mapping based on the various repeat groups that are present in your genome.</mark>

To create the more complex style Kimura plot for a single genome, create a file called *kimura-multi-complex.R* and copy and paste the below code into it:


```{r, eval=FALSE}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #
# 6-species Kimura plot
# - Centered titles
# - Only Schistocerca gregaria has y-axis label
# - Only Schistocerca nitens has x-axis label
# - Shared legend
# - Adds axis labels manually to avoid pivoting issues
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(cowplot)
  library(patchwork)
  library(readr)
  library(scales)
})

# ------------------- Species/files & genome sizes ---------------------------------------------- #
species_files <- list(
  "Schistocerca gregaria" = "song_assembly/SchGreg.distance",
  "Schistocerca serialis cubense" = "song_assembly/SchSeri.distance",
  "Schistocerca piceifrons" = "song_assembly/SchPice.distance",
  "Schistocerca cancellata" = "song_assembly/SchCanc.distance",
  "Schistocerca nitens" = "song_assembly/SchNite.distance",
  "Schistocerca americana" = "song_assembly/SchAmer.distance"
)

genome_sizes <- c(
  "Schistocerca gregaria" = 8700000000,
  "Schistocerca serialis cubense" = 9100000000,
  "Schistocerca piceifrons" = 8700000000,
  "Schistocerca cancellata" = 8500000000,
  "Schistocerca nitens" = 8800000000,
  "Schistocerca americana" = 9000000000
)

subfamily_palette <- c(
  # ---------------- DNA (blue → purple → green, more purples, muted) ----------------
  "DNA" = "#c8d9e8",
  "DNA/Academ-1" = "#b0c6db",
  "DNA/CMC-Chapaev-3" = "#99b3ce",
  "DNA/CMC-EnSpm" = "#8aa0d9",   # added purple tone
  "DNA/CMC-Transib" = "#7a8ddd", # added purple tone
  "DNA/Kolobok-Hydra" = "#6b7adf",# added purple tone
  "DNA/MULE-MuDR" = "#5c66e2",
  "DNA/MULE-NOF" = "#4d53e5",
  "DNA/Maverick" = "#3e40e8",
  "DNA/Merlin" = "#5340c5",      # darker purple
  "DNA/P" = "#6a5fce",
  "DNA/PIF-Harbinger" = "#807ecd",
  "DNA/PiggyBac" = "#968dd1",
  "DNA/Sola-1" = "#ac9dd4",
  "DNA/Sola-2" = "#c2add8",
  "DNA/TcMar-Fot1" = "#b7d8c2",  # muted green starts
  "DNA/TcMar-Mariner" = "#92c9a0",
  "DNA/TcMar-Pogo" = "#6ebb7e",
  "DNA/TcMar-Tc1" = "#4aa95c",
  "DNA/TcMar-Tc2" = "#369044",
  "DNA/TcMar-Tc4" = "#246a2f",
  "DNA/TcMar-Tigger" = "#134f1d",

  # ---------------- LINE (red → orange, muted) ----------------
  "LINE" = "#f4c8c8",
  "LINE/CR1" = "#eaa5a5",
  "LINE/Dong-R4" = "#e18383",
  "LINE/I" = "#d96161",
  "LINE/I-Jockey" = "#cf3f3f",
  "LINE/L1-Tx1" = "#b73535",
  "LINE/L2" = "#992b2b",
  "LINE/Penelope" = "#f4b38c",
  "LINE/R1" = "#e99f6a",
  "LINE/R1-LOA" = "#df8552",
  "LINE/R2" = "#d46a3b",
  "LINE/RTE-BovB" = "#ca5030",
  "LINE/RTE-RTE" = "#b84028",
  "LINE/RTE-X" = "#a12f20",
  "LINE/Rex-Babar" = "#892818",

  # ---------------- LTR (pink gradient, muted) ----------------
  "LTR" = "#f4d8e8",
  "LTR/Copia" = "#eaa5c9",
  "LTR/Gypsy" = "#e183ac",
  "LTR/Pao" = "#d9618f",

  # ---------------- RC/Helitron (muted orange) ----------------
  "RC/Helitron" = "#a002c7",

  # ---------------- Simple repeats (muted black/gray) ----------------
  "Simple_repeat" = "#2e2e2e",

  # ---------------- Unknown (muted gray) ----------------
  "Unknown" = "#8c8c8c",

  # ---------------- tRNA (muted yellow) ----------------
  "tRNA" = "#f4d966"
)






# ------------------- Function to create a basic plot without axis labels ------------------------- #
make_kimura_plot <- function(species, file, size) {
  # Read table safely
  df <- read_table(file, guess_max = 10000, na = c("", "NA"))

  # Remove empty trailing columns
  df <- df[, colSums(!is.na(df)) > 0]

  if (!"Div" %in% colnames(df)) stop(paste("Div column missing in file:", file))

  # Convert numeric columns
  df$Div <- as.numeric(df$Div)
  num_cols <- setdiff(colnames(df), "Div")
  df[num_cols] <- lapply(df[num_cols], as.numeric)

  df_long <- df %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = -Div, names_to = "Sub-family", values_to = "value", values_drop_na = TRUE) %>%
    filter(value > 0) %>%
    mutate(
      percentage_subfamily = (value / size) * 100,
      `Sub-family` = factor(str_trim(`Sub-family`), levels = names(subfamily_palette))
    )

  if (nrow(df_long) == 0) return(ggplot() + ggtitle(species) + theme_void())

  ggplot(df_long, aes(x = Div, y = percentage_subfamily, fill = `Sub-family`)) +
    geom_bar(position = "stack", stat = "identity", alpha = 0.8) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = subfamily_palette, drop = FALSE) +
    labs(title = species, x = "", y = "") +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = label_number(suffix = "%"), limits = c(0, 5)) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 50, 5), limits = c(0, 55), expand = c(0,0)) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 51)) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold.italic", hjust = 0.5),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.position = "none",
      plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 0, b = 30, l = 40)
    )
}

# ------------------- Generate 6 plots ----------------------------------------------------------- #
plots <- list(
  make_kimura_plot("Schistocerca gregaria", species_files[["Schistocerca gregaria"]], genome_sizes["Schistocerca gregaria"]),
  make_kimura_plot("Schistocerca serialis cubense", species_files[["Schistocerca serialis cubense"]], genome_sizes["Schistocerca serialis cubense"]),
  make_kimura_plot("Schistocerca piceifrons", species_files[["Schistocerca piceifrons"]], genome_sizes["Schistocerca piceifrons"]),
  make_kimura_plot("Schistocerca cancellata", species_files[["Schistocerca cancellata"]], genome_sizes["Schistocerca cancellata"]),
  make_kimura_plot("Schistocerca nitens", species_files[["Schistocerca nitens"]], genome_sizes["Schistocerca nitens"]),
  make_kimura_plot("Schistocerca americana", species_files[["Schistocerca americana"]], genome_sizes["Schistocerca americana"])
)

# ------------------- Shared legend ------------------------------------------------------------- #
legend_plot <- ggplot() +
  geom_bar(aes(x = 1, y = 1, fill = names(subfamily_palette)), stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = subfamily_palette, name = "Sub-family", guide = guide_legend(ncol = 1)) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

shared_legend <- get_legend(legend_plot)

# ------------------- Combine plots ------------------------------------------------------------- #
combined_plot <- wrap_plots(plots, ncol = 2, nrow = 3, guides = "collect")

final_plot <- plot_grid(
  combined_plot, shared_legend,
  rel_widths = c(3, 1),
  align = "hv"
)

# ------------------- Add manual axis labels ----------------------------------------------- #
final_plot_with_labels <- ggdraw(final_plot) +
  draw_label("Genome percentage", x = 0.03, y = 0.83, angle = 90, fontface = "bold", size = 16) +  # y-axis on left
  draw_label("Kimura substitution level", x = 0.22, y = 0.023, fontface = "bold", size = 16)       # x-axis at bottom

# ------------------- Save to PDF --------------------------------------------------------------- #
pdf("6_species_kimura_manual_labels.pdf", width = 16, height = 18)
print(final_plot_with_labels)
dev.off()


```
